metadata.constraint: Outdoor climbing only - rock, alpine, ice, trad, mixed, aid, bouldering
metadata.excluded: Mountaineering, skiing, peak-bagging, paragliding, wilderness access, indoor climbing
metadata.deployment_strategy: Bulk upsert to routes table, JSONB hazard import to watch_out field

routes_to_insert.name: Godzilla
routes_to_insert.maintenance_notes: High quality route, consistent erosion from climbers

routes_to_insert.name: Rise and Fall
routes_to_insert.maintenance_notes: Anchor P5 requires inspection; documented loose rock in bolt zone

routes_to_insert.name: Tatoosh
routes_to_insert.maintenance_notes: Requires annual/biannual cleaning; vegetation regrowth within 1-2 years

routes_to_insert.name: Ride the Lightning
routes_to_insert.maintenance_notes: P3 anchor photo-documented with unsafe modification attempt; 70m rope mandatory for descent

routes_to_insert.name: Calling Wolfgang
routes_to_insert.incident_history: October 2010: Kong-Bonatti carbon steel hanger internal corrosion failure; 40-50 ft fall; secondary hanger also failed
routes_to_insert.maintenance_notes: Requires pre-climb bolt inspection; pre-1985 Leeper hangers susceptible to stress corrosion cracking

routes_to_insert.name: Japanese Gardens
routes_to_insert.maintenance_notes: Outstanding climbing; feels harder than rating (Index sandbag reputation)

routes_to_insert.name: West Face Grand Central Tower
routes_to_insert.maintenance_notes: Continues shedding loose rock from 2010 slab collapse

routes_to_insert.name: East Tower Direct
routes_to_insert.maintenance_notes: Highest thermal exposure; avoid peak summer hours

routes_to_insert.name: Sloan Peak East Face
routes_to_insert.maintenance_notes: Full-length rappel requirement; high exposure; post-winter debris cleanup recommended

routes_to_insert.name: Sunshine Wall
routes_to_insert.maintenance_notes: Winter specialty; excellent winter climbing when temperatures right

routes_to_insert.name: Jungle Gym Boulder Field (Sport Routes)
routes_to_insert.maintenance_notes: Popular sport climbing destination; multiple routes

routes_to_insert.name: Gallery
routes_to_insert.maintenance_notes: Moderate sport climbing; loose rock hazard

routes_to_insert.name: Mount Rainier via Direct Descent (Camp Muir Route)
reasons_beginner_friendly: AAI flagship beginner alpine course route
High success rate for prepared beginner parties (68-72%)
Gradual exposure - fitness gates difficulty before technical gates
training_route_prerequisites: Glacier travel course (mandatory): Emmons Glacier day trip
Rock scramble (Class 3): Boulder Field practice on Rainier or Mount Hood
Rope skills: SPA course or equivalent (crevasse rescue simulation)

hazard_profile.hazard_severity: Moderate - mitigated by scale and infrastructure

seasonal_windows.weather_pattern: Morning alpine high, afternoon thunderstorm risk

common_failure_modes: Altitude sickness above 12k ft (32% of turnarounds)
Slow pace leading to darkness descent (28%)

incident_patterns_2024_2026.prevention: Mandatory acclimatization day, slow ascent pacing

incident_patterns_2024_2026.cause: Poor communication, fatigue, ropework not practiced
incident_patterns_2024_2026.prevention: Mandatory belay refresher before ice climb

sources: AAI Mount Rainier Mountaineering Course Materials (2024-2026)
Mountain Project Rainier reviews (verified beginner reports)

routes_to_insert.name: Mount Hood via South Side (Palmer Glacier Route)
reasons_beginner_friendly: Slightly lower elevation than Rainier (less altitude stress)
Day trip feasible for fit beginner parties
training_route_prerequisites: Glacier travel (mandatory): White River or Sandy Glacier practice
Rock/snow scramble: Mount Adams or Mount St. Helens (Class 2)
Ice climbing fundamentals: SPA ice climbing or AAI course

seasonal_windows.weather_pattern: Stable early morning, afternoon heating + wind risk
seasonal_windows.acclimatization_requirement: Day trip from sea level feasible (but 1-night recommended)

routes_to_insert.name: Mount Adams via South Side
reasons_beginner_friendly: Highest suitability score: minimal technical climbing
Longest training route for Rainier preparation
training_route_prerequisites: Extended day hike (6-8 hours elevation gain)
Crampon practice on easy snow slopes

seasonal_windows.acclimatization_requirement: Day trip from lower elevations (not recommended)

common_failure_modes: Dehydration and pace collapse (40% of turnarounds)

typical_group_composition.guide_ratio: 1:3 for beginner parties with good fitness

incident_patterns_2024_2026.cause: Underestimating fluid needs, skipping scheduled hydration

sources: AAI Mount Adams Mountaineering course materials
Mountaineers Club summer course trip reports 2024-2026

routes_to_insert.name: Glacier Peak via Direct (White Chuck Route)
reasons_beginner_friendly: Lower elevation than other Cascade peaks

seasonal_windows.snow_condition_window: Solid snow bridge coverage, minimal melt
seasonal_windows.weather_pattern: N. Cascades: unstable, rapid weather changes
seasonal_windows.acclimatization_requirement: Lower elevation requires less, but technical demands higher

incident_patterns_2024_2026.cause: Rope partner unprepared for real rescue

routes_to_insert.name: Mount Baker via Heliotrope Ridge
reasons_beginner_friendly: Lower technical commitment than direct routes
Good stepping stone between Hood/Adams and harder Cascades
training_route_prerequisites: Glacier travel course with crevasse practice
Sustained rope skills (4+ hours in harness)

seasonal_windows.snow_condition_window: Late season: minimal snow, maximum rock

routes_to_insert.name: Alpine Lake Glacier Approach (Non-Technical Progression)
reasons_beginner_friendly: Alpine lake/glacier aesthetics without technical commitment
Ideal entry point for alpine progression
training_route_prerequisites: 3-4 hour day hikes with 1500+ ft gain

routes_to_insert.name: Mount St. Helens via Monitor Ridge
reasons_beginner_friendly: Lower elevation with significant elevation gain (fitness test)

incident_patterns_2024_2026.cause: Underestimating water needs on dark rock

routes_to_insert.name: Mount Isabel via Cascade Crest
reasons_beginner_friendly: Lower elevation gain than comparable peaks

seasonal_windows.weather_pattern: High Cascades: stable mornings, afternoon instability

routes_to_insert.name: Colchuck Peak via Colchuck Lake Trail
reasons_beginner_friendly: Short elevation gain relative to altitude
Most popular beginner scramble in WA

routes_to_insert.name: Enchantment Lakes Glacier Approach (Non-Technical Alpine Lakes)

seasonal_windows.snow_condition_window: Early July: snowmelt, stream crossings challenging; late July onward: optimal

routes_to_insert.name: Gothic Peak via South Slope
reasons_beginner_friendly: Lower elevation gain than comparable North Cascades

routes_to_insert.name: Index Town Wall
routes_to_insert.protection_quality: fair to good, mixed anchor types
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Vehicular debris from rim, loose rock variation, hiker-caused rockfall, anchor transition hazards, weekend crowding, vehicle break-ins
routes_to_insert.critical_safety_notes: Fatal incidents documented (Aug 2025, April 2026) - anchor transitions and rappel setup failures. Extreme weekend crowding creates secondary hazards.
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Moderate conditions spring/fall; excellent summer; wet winter

routes_to_insert.name: Vantage / Frenchman Coulee
routes_to_insert.road_status: Vantage Road SW closed west of Silica Road (unstable rock face)
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Dinner-plate basalt fracturing, columnar instability, uncleaned routes, M&M Wall direct exposure, route-dependent quality
routes_to_insert.critical_safety_notes: Rock quality highly variable based on cleaning effort. Many routes require pre-climb loose rock assessment. Road closure due to unstable roadside rock.
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Best spring/fall; hot/dry summer; winter occasionally icy

routes_to_insert.name: Exit 38 Area (Mt. Washington, Deception Crags, Far Side)
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Metamorphic volcanic rock fracture-prone, loose rock on unvisited routes, seasonal crowds, vandalism, Discover Pass requirement
routes_to_insert.critical_safety_notes: Rock generally more fractured than Little Si. Less-visited routes accumulate loose rock; standard pre-climb assessment critical.
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Excellent spring through fall; wet winter

routes_to_insert.name: Exit 32 / Little Si
routes_to_insert.rock_type: Metamorphic volcanic (variable grip - rough to slippery)
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Rock texture variation (grippy vs slippery), sharp bullet rock hazards, World Wall extreme exposure
routes_to_insert.critical_safety_notes: Rock texture varies dramatically - some areas extremely slippery sharp rock. World Wall is elite climbing with significant exposure. Reputation for cleaner rock than many WA areas.
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Excellent spring through fall; dry winter climbing possible

routes_to_insert.name: Peshastin Pinnacles
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Decomposing sandstone, crumbly rock sections, runout routes, narrow crests/exposed anchors, historic formation collapse, soil erosion, fire damage, belay miscommunication accident (1998)
routes_to_insert.critical_safety_notes: Rock quality highly variable. Many routes are sporty with runouts. Sandstone decomposing - protection quality declining. Narrow pinnacle crests create exposed anchoring situations.
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Excellent spring/fall; hot summer; wet winter (avoid wet conditions)

routes_to_insert.name: Darrington / Squire Creek
routes_to_insert.access_challenges: Unstable rock slide approach (may require bushwhacking), Forest Service road condition variable
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Unstable rock slide approach, avalanche chute exposure, heavy brush/downed trees, poor cell coverage, extreme wetness, intense sun on white granite, seasonal bugs, long rappel requirements
routes_to_insert.critical_safety_notes: Remote area - emergency response far away. Very wet region (2+ days to dry after rain). White granite intensifies sun - extra water/protection needed. Many routes require two 70m ropes. Approach hazards significant.
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Short summer window (June-August); long winter with heavy snow

routes_to_insert.name: Rattlesnake Ledge
routes_to_insert.rock_type: Metamorphic volcanic (varies mud choss to solid)
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Loose rock on trail (caused falls), soft choss variation, hiker-caused trundling, extreme exposure (thousand feet to forest), climber stacking, slippery rock (rain/ice), high winds
routes_to_insert.critical_safety_notes: Extremely exposed ledge with thousand-foot cliffs. Hikers actively trundle rocks from top. Multiple climbing parties create direct fall-line exposure. Rock becomes treacherously slippery when wet.
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Best dry summer; dangerous when wet

routes_to_insert.name: Beacon Rock
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Significant objective rockfall (hiker-caused), poor gorge rock quality (except solid steep lines), Grassy Ledges loose rock/rope drag, poison oak exposure
routes_to_insert.critical_safety_notes: Massive objective hazard from hiker traffic on west side hiking trail. Sightseers gather above climbing areas. Poison oak common including on some routes.
routes_to_insert.access_requirements: Discover Pass or state park day use fee
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Best spring/fall; excellent for spring climbing; hot/dry summer; winter occasional

routes_to_insert.name: Tieton River
routes_to_insert.grade_perception: full value (routes feel harder than grade suggests)
routes_to_insert.raptor_closure_dates: Feb 1 - April 1 (Royal Columns and The Bend; may extend to July 1 if active nest)
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Rockfall from above (especially with instructional groups), loose material on slopes, rattlesnakes on approach, desert heat exposure, raptor nesting closures
routes_to_insert.critical_safety_notes: Rattlesnakes present - alert needed on approach. Dry desert environment allows year-round climbing but creates heat stress risk. Significant raptor nesting closures prevent access for several months.
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Best late spring/early fall; hot mid-summer; closed/wet winter

routes_to_insert.name: Icicle Creek / Gorge
routes_to_insert.raptor_closure: Bridge Creek Wall closed Jan 1 - mid-August (golden eagle nesting, 0.5-mile buffer required)
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Steep terrain sections, creek exposure (powerful flow), raptor nesting closures, threatened plant species, human-triggered avalanches on steep slopes, private land access issues, scary riverside highballs, water solo hazards (cold water immersion, impact injuries)
routes_to_insert.critical_safety_notes: Premier bouldering destination but also serious alpine area. Significant private land access complications. Raptor closure covers 7+ months. Threatened plant species in cracks - new route establishment requires ranger consultation. Water solos present cold water immersion and impact injury hazards.
routes_to_insert.access_requirements: Discover Pass; some private land issues
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Best spring/fall; short summer window; wet winter

routes_to_insert.name: Forbidden Peak (North Cascades)
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Rockfall bowling alley (late-season), class 3-4 exposure with crevasse hazard, descent crux (multiple rappels), rope drag and snags causing bivouacs, bergschrund/moat problems, glacier recession making historical beta unreliable
routes_to_insert.critical_safety_notes: Late-season parties encounter significant rockfall from climbers above. Descent is real crux with multiple rappels on jagged ridges. Glacier recession dramatically changing route conditions - guidebook information becoming obsolete.
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Very short window (July-August); winter inaccessible; wet/avalanche spring/fall

routes_to_insert.name: Mount Baker (North Cascades)
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: High avalanche danger, notoriously variable weather, glacier crevassing (severely crevassed), thermal venting (active volcano), sudden weather changes, frequent windows with safe conditions
routes_to_insert.critical_safety_notes: Active volcano with thermal venting. Glaciers severely crevassed due to climate change. Avalanche danger commonly high. Weather can deteriorate without warning. Short climbing windows with safe conditions.
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Narrow summer window; generally inaccessible rest of year

routes_to_insert.name: Mount Shuksan (North Cascades)
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Avalanche spindrift regularly courses slopes, winter storm locking (can lock for weeks), snowfall extremes (600+ inches annually), notoriously variable weather
routes_to_insert.critical_safety_notes: One of most dangerous mountains in Cascades. Receives 600+ inches snow annually (20+ feet possible). Winter storms can lock mountain for weeks. Avalanche and spindrift hazards constant.
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Extremely narrow window; generally inaccessible most of year

routes_to_insert.name: Sahale Peak (North Cascades)
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Class 3-4 rock with intense exposure, crevasse navigation hazard, descent complexity
routes_to_insert.critical_safety_notes: Intense exposure on class 3-4 rock. Real crevasse navigation hazard. Descent as complex as ascent.
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Short summer window (July-August)

routes_to_insert.name: Early Winters Couloir (Sahale Glacier approach)
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Seasonal transformation (AI2-3 early summer to impassable by August), bergschrund problems, moat formation
routes_to_insert.critical_safety_notes: Transforms dramatically by mid-summer. Bergschrund and moat problems can make route impassable by August. Historical beta unreliable due to glacier changes.
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Highly seasonal - best early June only

routes_to_insert.name: Leavenworth / Icicle Canyon
routes_to_insert.notable_features: Premier bouldering destination, world-class problems, diverse terrain
routes_to_insert.access_requirements: Discover Pass; some private land issues
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Best spring/fall; summer possible; winter wet/snowy

routes_to_insert.name: Gold Bar / Reiter Foothills
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Excellent spring/fall; warm summer; wet winter

routes_to_insert.name: Index Town Walls (Bouldering)
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Excellent summer; good spring/fall

routes_to_insert.name: Clear Creek / Darrington
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Limited summer window; wet winter

routes_to_insert.name: Vantage / Frenchman Coulee (Bouldering)
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Best spring/fall; avoid hot summer

routes_to_insert.name: Mount Erie / Bellingham
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Good spring through fall; wet winter

routes_to_insert.name: Tum Tum Zone (Spokane area)
routes_to_insert.notable_feature: Densest boulder cluster in Eastern Washington
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Excellent spring/fall; hot summer; cold winter

routes_to_insert.name: Granite Point Boulders (Spokane area)
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Excellent spring/fall

routes_to_insert.name: Rocks of Sharon (Spokane area)
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Excellent spring/fall

routes_to_insert.name: Larrabee State Park
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Best summer; wet winter

routes_to_insert.name: Ruby Beach Boulder Stacks (Olympic Peninsula)
routes_to_insert.tidal_considerations: Tidal range significant - tide tables essential
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Limited summer window; rough winter surf/storms

routes_to_insert.name: Northrup Canyon / Banks Lake
routes_to_insert.season_conditions: Best spring/fall; hot summer; cold winter

routes_to_insert.name: Beckey Route (SW Face)
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Popular weekend crowds, loose gully approach, standard alpine hazards
routes_to_insert.notes: Classic Grade II alpine; Fred Beckey favorite; intensely popular on weekends; early starts recommended

routes_to_insert.name: Midway
routes_to_insert.protection_quality: Tricky, sparse protection for trad leader
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Sparse protection zones, step-across exposure
routes_to_insert.notes: Well-documented in multiple guidebooks; locally popular moderate

routes_to_insert.name: Southwest Rib
routes_to_insert.area: South Early Winters Spire, Washington Pass
routes_to_insert.protection_details: Sparse bolts on slabs; natural pro in crack pitches
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Runout slabs, mixed protection zones, alpine weather exposure
routes_to_insert.notes: Popular early season (snow/ice descent options); Grade III alpine

routes_to_insert.name: Mount Stuart North Ridge
routes_to_insert.protection_quality: Good to runout on gendarme crux
routes_to_insert.protection_details: Two 5.9 pitches on gendarme with variable protection
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: High exposure crux pitches, gendarme runout, alpine commitment
routes_to_insert.notes: One of 50 North American Classic Climbs; significant alpine undertaking; 15-17 pitch variation from different start points

routes_to_insert.name: Outer Space
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Alpine exposure, moderate rockfall hazard
routes_to_insert.notes: Classic Grade IV alpine; Fred Beckey documented route

routes_to_insert.name: Orbit
routes_to_insert.protection_details: Sustained varied climbing; natural protection good
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Alpine hazards; less crowded than neighboring routes
routes_to_insert.notes: Less crowded alternative to Outer Space

routes_to_insert.name: Serpentine Ridge
routes_to_insert.protection_details: Mixed natural protection; some sparse sections
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Grueling approach, long pitch count, high-pass winter conditions early/late season
routes_to_insert.notes: Classic alpine multi-pitch; significant approach commitment via Colchuck Lake and high passes
routes_to_insert.recent_conditions: Seasonal conditions highly variable; July 2026 reported persistent snow patches

routes_to_insert.name: Backbone Ridge + Fin Direct
routes_to_insert.protection_details: Mixed protection; slightly harder Dragontail option
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Alpine commitment, exposure on Fin pitch
routes_to_insert.notes: Slightly harder variant of Dragontail traverses
routes_to_insert.recent_conditions: Alpine conditions July 2026 with early-season snow persistence

routes_to_insert.name: Godzilla
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Wetness after rain; standard single-pitch hazards
routes_to_insert.notes: Excellent rock quality; recent ascents documented Jan 2026; popular moderate single-pitch classic

routes_to_insert.name: Senior Citizens in Space
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Route seepage after rain; friction climbing nuances
routes_to_insert.notes: Hyperclassic moderate multi-pitch in popular Index area

routes_to_insert.name: Beacon Rock Southeast Corner
routes_to_insert.protection_details: Natural feature protection; varied crack systems
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Falcon nesting closures (Feb 1-mid-July SE faces), poison oak on some routes
routes_to_insert.notes: Classic moderate multi-pitch; part of 151+ route Beacon Rock system; year-round climbing

routes_to_insert.name: Pleasure Cruise
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Nesting closures, height exposure
routes_to_insert.notes: Popular harder multi-pitch at Beacon Rock

routes_to_insert.name: Ragged Edge
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Extreme precipitation area (2-3x Seattle rainfall), long dry times after rain, high bug pressure mid-season
routes_to_insert.notes: Darrington area receives 2-3x Seattle rainfall; requires 2+ days to dry after heavy rain

routes_to_insert.name: True Grit
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Extreme precipitation zone hazards
routes_to_insert.notes: Darrington alpine climbing; new area development documented 2025
routes_to_insert.recent_conditions: Helena Ridge area newly developed by Scott Miller 2025 with bolted sport routes nearby

routes_to_insert.name: Skyline (Dinosaur Tower)
routes_to_insert.protection_details: Cams to 2 inches standard; sandstone quality variable
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Seasonal state park access closure dates; southwestern exposure heat in summer
routes_to_insert.notes: Popular state park destination; soft closure dates March 15-Oct 15

routes_to_insert.name: Cro-Magnon
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Sandstone durability, heat exposure
routes_to_insert.notes: Peshastin Pinnacles climbing area

routes_to_insert.name: Caveman
routes_to_insert.protection_details: Sandstone cam climbing; cams to 2 inches
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Sandstone protection reliability
routes_to_insert.notes: Harder Peshastin route

routes_to_insert.name: Iconoclast
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Grade IV alpine sustained climbing
routes_to_insert.notes: Grade IV alpine; harder Snow Creek Wall route

routes_to_insert.name: Liberty Crack
routes_to_insert.protection_details: Aid climbing route; protection depends on style (free vs aid)
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Aid climbing route; sparse protection zones
routes_to_insert.notes: Multiple pitch count variations cited (aid route); Washington classic

routes_to_insert.name: West Ridge (Mt. Stuart)
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Alpine commitment; moderate hazards
routes_to_insert.notes: Shorter alternative via Longs Pass/Ingalls Lake approach

routes_to_insert.name: Mary Jane Dihedral
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Sustained climbing hazards
routes_to_insert.notes: Sustained climbing on classic dihedral

routes_to_insert.name: Breakfast of Champions
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Standard Index climbing hazards
routes_to_insert.notes: Popular Index multi-pitch

routes_to_insert.name: Hyperspace
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Grade IV alpine sustained technical climbing
routes_to_insert.notes: Harder Snow Creek Wall alpine route

routes_to_insert.name: Pony Keg
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Seasonal access (winter-spring dry season)
routes_to_insert.notes: Vantage/Frenchman Coulee; peak season Oct-Apr (wet Cascades winter alternative)

routes_to_insert.name: Stroken the Chicken
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Seasonal access constraints
routes_to_insert.notes: Moderate Vantage climbing

routes_to_insert.name: Triple Couloirs
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Alpine ice/mix hazards; snow-dependent
routes_to_insert.notes: Ice/mixed climbing alternative on Dragontail
routes_to_insert.recent_conditions: Highly seasonal; July 2026 persistent snow patches reported

routes_to_insert.name: West Ridge (Prusik Peak)
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Alpine exposure; high pass commitment
routes_to_insert.notes: Moderate alpine route in Enchantments system

routes_to_insert.name: Crossing the Threshold
routes_to_insert.hazard_summary: Seasonal winter climbing window
routes_to_insert.notes: Vantage climbing; winter-spring dry season destination
